subject: Project Management
title: Retrospectives
description: A short lesson to help you apply Retrospectives.

hook.text: At the end of every sprint, Agile teams ask: 'What went well? What didn't? What do we change?'

buildup.text: The retrospective is the engine of continuous improvement — the team reflecting on itself.

discovery.text: Teams that skip retros repeat the same mistakes. Teams that hold them get measurably better.

twist.text: The hardest part isn't identifying problems — it's creating safety for honest conversation.

climax.text: The retrospective is Agile's most important ceremony — and the most often skipped.

punchline.text: The meeting that improves all other meetings.

quiz.question: What is the purpose of an Agile retrospective?
options: To reflect on what went well and what to improve
To plan the next sprint's tasks
